source.page_title: Work on the Water in Vancouver with False Creek Ferries
source.validation_reason: Career language detected, but job state is unclear
source.body_text_sample:
Employment
Join Our Team

If you have customer service experience & know how to handle a small boat, we want to hear from you!

Because we take safety very seriously at False Creek Ferries, we will only consider applicants that have already completed the necessary certification. We understand that obtaining the SVOP & SDV-BS licenses constitute a significant undertaking for prospective candidates. We are happy to consider resumes from those who don’t yet have the licences, but are planning on getting them, for shore based positions. These licenses are required by Transport Canada.

Available Positions

We are currently hiring for various positions for the Spring / Summer season. Please forward your resume with attached copies of all relevant certificates and references.

Ferry Operator
Deckhand
Dock Crew (Summer)
SEND RESUME
General Requirements
Presentable, energetic & physically fit
Able to work well independently, and as part of a team
Experienced in handling small boats
Exceptional customer service ability
Excellent English language skills (other languages an asset)
Able & willing to work long hours, various shifts, and available 7 days a week
Experience handling cash, bondable
Additional Assets
Experience working in the tourism industry; public speaking or tour guide experience
Experience working with diesel engines
Experience in boat maintenance in wood or fibreglass
Required Certifications
Ferry Operators
Small Vessel Operator Proficiency (SVOP)
Basic Safety (SDV-BS Fo
